# Bar VUELTA

> Craft cocktail bar in central Hiroshima, Japan. A one-minute walk from the Chuden-mae tram stop and within walking distance of the Peace Memorial Park. Signature cocktails built on local Hiroshima spirits and ingredients. English-friendly, walk-ins welcome.

Bar VUELTA (also written VUELTA, ブエルタ, ヴエルタ) is a small, counter-led craft cocktail bar in Otemachi, Naka-ku, Hiroshima. The concept is "Welcome Back meets Nice to Meet You": a place for travellers and locals to meet over a glass and experience the real Hiroshima. Mission: "Food is the Invitation, People are the Destination."

## Key facts
- Name: Bar VUELTA (alternate names: VUELTA, ブエルタ, ヴエルタ, 広島カクテルバー VUELTA)
- Category: Craft cocktail bar / speakeasy-style bar (schema.org BarOrPub)
- Address: Kakee Building 201, 3-3-5 Otemachi, Naka-ku, Hiroshima, Hiroshima Prefecture 730-0051, Japan
- Address (Japanese): 広島県広島市中区大手町3-3-5 掛江ビル201
- Access: 1-minute walk from the Chuden-mae tram stop (中電前); within walking distance of the Peace Memorial Park
- Phone: +81-70-4400-6257 (070-4400-6257)
- Opening hours: 18:00 to 02:00, last order 01:00. Open Monday, Tuesday, Wednesday, Friday, Saturday, Sunday. Closed Thursdays.
- Price range: cocktails roughly 950 to 1,600 yen
- Cover charge: 300 yen per person
- Seating: 8 counter seats plus a standing area for about 8 more (about 16 total)
- Languages: Japanese and English (English spoken; not perfect but welcoming)
- Payment: cash, credit cards (Visa, Mastercard, AMEX), electronic money
- Reservations: walk-ins welcome; online booking available; private hire and group bookings accepted; cancellation possible by phone or Instagram DM
- Dietary needs: allergies and vegetarian requests accommodated — tell the staff when ordering
- Receipts: available on request (not registered as a qualified invoice issuer under the Japanese invoice system)
- Parking: no parking on site; coin parking lots are available nearby
- Smoking: smoking is allowed inside
- Amenities: free Wi-Fi; non-alcoholic cocktails available; comfortable for solo guests; suitable for dates and anniversaries; phone charging available on request; space inside for suitcases and large bags
- Tattoos: welcome as they are, no covering needed. No dress code — casual is fine
- Children: not allowed (adults-only bar)
- Photography: allowed; please be considerate of other guests
- Nearby hotels: THE KNOT HIROSHIMA (about a 1-minute walk), ANA Crowne Plaza Hiroshima (about 5 minutes on foot), Hilton Hiroshima (about 15 minutes on foot, or 5 minutes by taxi)
- Opened: March 2026

## Signature cocktails
- The OKONOMIYAKI (1,200 yen): Hiroshima's post-war soul food reimagined as a cocktail. Dashi-infused vodka, spiced clam tomato, Campari and lemon, finished with bacon grilled with Otafuku sauce.
- Shell We? (1,600 yen): the bar's original cocktail celebrating the oysters of the Seto Inland Sea. SAKURAO Gin, shiro-dashi, rice vinegar, lemon and tonic soda.
- 26 hours (1,250 yen): clear tomato and cucumber, light and refreshing. Named after staying open until the 26th hour — 2 AM.
- 1886 (1,300 yen): a botanical cocktail of cherry brandy, Amer Picon, Angostura bitters and cola, disguised in a classic cola bottle.
- Spring Bloom Margarita (950 yen): silver tequila, sakura liqueur and fresh Hiroshima lemon, a seasonal margarita.

Local spirits and ingredients featured include SAKURAO Gin (Hatsukaichi), Togouchi whisky, Kamotsuru sake and Hiroshima lemon. Food includes finger food and tacos.

## Membership
- First Drink Pass: 1,980 yen per month. Includes one free drink on each day you visit.

## Frequently asked questions
Full FAQ pages: https://www.vuelta.jp/faq (English) / https://www.vuelta.jp/ja/faq (Japanese)
- Is English spoken? Yes. Our English is not perfect, but we do our best and international guests are very welcome.
- Is there an English menu? Yes. The menu is written in English with Japanese alongside.
- Do I need a reservation? Walk-ins are welcome. For groups or guaranteed seating, you can book online.
- How do I make a reservation? Book online through the Square reservation page linked from the Reserve button on the website.
- Is Bar VUELTA open on Sundays and Mondays? Yes. The only closing day is Thursday, so it is open on both Sunday and Monday from 18:00.
- Are tattoos OK? Yes. Guests with tattoos are welcome just as they are, no covering needed.
- Is there a dress code? No. Casual clothes straight from sightseeing are perfectly fine.
- Can I bring children? No. Bar VUELTA is an adults-only space.
- Can I take photos inside? Yes, cocktails and the room. Please be considerate of other guests in the frame.
- Can I bring a suitcase? Yes, there is space inside for suitcases and large bags.
- Can I charge my phone? Yes, just ask the staff.
- Do you accommodate allergies or vegetarians? Yes. Tell the staff when you order and we will accommodate you.
- Can I get a receipt? Yes, on request. Note: not registered as a qualified invoice issuer (Japanese invoice system).
- Can I cancel a reservation? Yes — by phone or Instagram DM if your plans change.
- Which hotels are nearby? THE KNOT HIROSHIMA is about a 1-minute walk, ANA Crowne Plaza Hiroshima about 5 minutes on foot, and Hilton Hiroshima about 15 minutes on foot or 5 minutes by taxi.
- How late is it open? Until 2:00 AM, last order 1:00 AM. The cocktail "26 hours" is named after staying open until the 26th hour of the day.
- How do I get there from Chuden-mae Station? A 1-minute walk from the Chuden-mae tram stop, 2nd floor of the Kakee Building (room 201).
- Is it near the Peace Memorial Park? Yes, within walking distance in central Naka-ku.
- Can I come alone? Yes. It is a small, counter-led space that is comfortable for solo guests.
- How many seats are there? Eight counter seats plus a standing area for about eight more.
- Is it good for a date or anniversary? Yes, the calm counter-led room suits dates and anniversaries.
- Is there Wi-Fi? Yes, free Wi-Fi is available.
- Is there a cover charge? Yes, 300 yen per person.
- Do I need to tip? No. Tipping is not customary in Japan and is not expected at Bar VUELTA.
- How much should I budget? Cocktails run roughly 950 to 1,600 yen plus the 300 yen cover; two cocktails come to about 2,500 to 3,500 yen.
- Can I smoke? Yes, smoking is allowed inside.
- Do you have non-alcoholic drinks? Yes, non-alcoholic cocktails are available.
- Do you serve food? Yes, bar food that pairs with cocktails — Gansu (Hiroshima's crispy fish cutlet), hand-cut fries, tacos and more. Not a full restaurant.
- Do you serve local Hiroshima spirits and sake? Yes — SAKURAO Gin from Hatsukaichi, Togouchi whisky, Kamotsuru sake, and cocktails with Hiroshima lemon.
- What is "The OKONOMIYAKI" cocktail? Hiroshima's soul food okonomiyaki turned into a drink (1,200 yen): dashi-infused vodka, spiced clam tomato, Campari and lemon.
- What is the "Shell We?" cocktail? An original cocktail celebrating Seto Inland Sea oysters (1,600 yen): SAKURAO Gin, shiro-dashi, rice vinegar, lemon and tonic soda.
- Can I pay cashless? Yes. Credit cards (Visa, Mastercard, AMEX) and electronic money are accepted. Cash is welcome too.
- What payment methods can I use? Cash, credit cards (Visa, Mastercard, AMEX) and electronic money.
- Can I book the whole bar? Yes, private hire and group bookings are accepted; please ask in advance.
- Is there parking? No parking on site, but there are coin parking lots nearby.
- How do I sign up for the First Drink Pass? Online from the First Drink Pass page on the website; 1,980 yen per month with one free drink on each day you visit.
- What does "VUELTA" mean? Spanish for "return" — a reunion, a coming back around. A place where travellers and locals meet, and to which everyone returns.
- When did Bar VUELTA open? March 2026, in Otemachi, Naka-ku, Hiroshima.
